issues.location_in_plan: §3 Activation site paragraph; Task 5 Goal paragraph; Task 5a Goal paragraph + implementation bullet 'Update app.py:441'
issues.claim: The plan states: 'Caretaker is invoked from create_app() after session engine construction and before any router is mounted. This is the same call site as initialize_session_schema at app.py:441' and 'Update app.py:441 to remove the initialize_session_schema call.'
issues.reality: Line 441 of src/elspeth/web/app.py is `app = FastAPI(title="ELSPETH Web", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)` — the FastAPI constructor call. The actual `initialize_session_schema(session_engine)` call is at line 581, inside the '--- Session database setup ---' block. The import of initialize_session_schema is at line 69. Task 5a's implementation bullet therefore targets the wrong line number; an implementer following the plan literally will edit the wrong location.
issues.resolution: Update all three references to 'app.py:441' to 'app.py:581' in §3, Task 5, and Task 5a.
issues.evidence: grep -n 'initialize_session_schema' src/elspeth/web/app.py → line 69 (import), line 581 (call); sed -n '441p' src/elspeth/web/app.py → 'app = FastAPI(title="ELSPETH Web", version="0.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)'

issues.location_in_plan: Task 5a Goal paragraph: 'It is a module-level function at schema.py:226 called from initialize_session_schema at line 60'
issues.claim: The plan states _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry is 'a module-level function at schema.py:226 called from initialize_session_schema at line 60'.
issues.reality: In src/elspeth/web/sessions/schema.py: initialize_session_schema is defined at line 75 (not line 60); _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry is defined at line 377 (not line 226); the call to _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry is at line 185 (inside initialize_session_schema). Line 226 of schema.py is inside _validate_columns, an unrelated helper. The file is 471 lines total.
issues.resolution: Correct all three cited line numbers in Task 5a: initialize_session_schema defined at line 75; _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry defined at line 377; called at line 185.

issues.location_in_plan: §2 Option (c) classification table; Task 1 Goal ('Walk sessions/models.py and add info to every Table definition'); Task 1 TDD shape ('asserts every Table in metadata.tables.values() has tbl.info["migration_class"] in {"data", "definitional"}')
issues.claim: The plan's classification table in §2 lists 13 tables to classify and implies this covers every Table in metadata. Task 1 asks the implementer to classify 'every existing table' per the §2 table.
issues.reality: src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py at HEAD (RC5.2, commit ca9bc05bd) defines 15 Table objects. The plan's §2 table is missing two: (1) composer_completion_events_table (variable) / 'composer_completion_events' (table name string) declared at line 719 — added by Phase 6 commit 93c374d63 which is in RC5.2's ancestry; (2) skill_markdown_history_table (variable) / 'skill_markdown_history' (table name string) declared at line 798. Phase 6 is not named anywhere in the plan and is not listed as a BLOCKED-on dependency in §8. An implementer following the §2 table exactly will leave two live tables without migration_class classification, which the caretaker will then default-classify as 'data' and emit classification-default warnings on every deploy — silently, without the operator knowing these warnings are spurious (the classification was just omitted from the plan).
issues.resolution: Add composer_completion_events_table and skill_markdown_history_table to the §2 classification table with appropriate class assignments (likely both 'data'). Add Phase 6 shipping to the §8 BLOCKED-on list alongside Phase 1A and Phase 5b. Update Task 1's TDD narrative to cover all 15 tables.
issues.evidence: grep -n '^[a-z_]*_table\s*=\s*Table' src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py → 15 Table definitions at lines 84, 172, 269, 371, 469, 506, 719, 798, 933, 1000, 1158, 1183, 1203, 1238, 1305; git log --oneline RC5.2 -- models.py → commit 93c374d63 'feat(web/sessions): add composer_completion_events_table' is in RC5.2 ancestry; commit ca9bc05bd (HEAD) contains both missing tables

issues.location_in_plan: Task 5a implementation bullet: exhaustive caller list (9 files enumerated)
issues.claim: Task 5a provides an exhaustive list of 9 test files calling initialize_session_schema that must be updated in the same commit as the deletion: tests/integration/web/conftest.py, tests/integration/web/composer/guided/test_step_handlers.py, tests/integration/web/test_blobs_ready_hash_postgres.py, tests/integration/web/test_compose_loop_latency_sanity.py, tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py, tests/integration/web/test_inv_audit_ahead_backward.py, tests/integration/web/composer/guided/test_progressive_disclosure.py, tests/integration/web/composer/guided/conftest.py, tests/integration/web/test_compose_loop_concurrent_sessions.py.
issues.reality: grep -rln initialize_session_schema tests/ returns 50 files across integration, unit, and property test directories. The plan's list of 9 is missing at least 41 additional callers, including entire test suites (all unit/web/sessions/ tests, all unit/web/composer/ tests, unit/web/secrets/ tests, unit/web/blobs/ tests, property/web/composer/ tests, and more). Selected examples missing from the plan's list: tests/integration/web/test_preferences_routes.py, tests/integration/web/test_tutorial_routes.py, tests/integration/web/composer/test_chat_messages_attributability.py, tests/integration/web/composer/test_inline_source_provenance.py, tests/integration/web/composer/test_interpretation_runtime_handoff.py, tests/unit/web/sessions/test_schema.py, tests/unit/web/sessions/test_models.py, tests/unit/web/composer/test_tools.py, tests/unit/web/composer/test_service.py, tests/unit/web/secrets/test_service.py, tests/unit/web/secrets/test_routes.py, tests/unit/web/conftest.py, tests/property/web/composer/conftest.py, tests/property/web/composer/test_compose_loop_invariants.py. Committing Task 5a with only the 9-file list would leave 41+ broken call sites.
issues.resolution: Remove the enumerated 9-file list from Task 5a and replace with the directive to run 'grep -rln initialize_session_schema tests/ src/' immediately before the commit to obtain the current exhaustive list. The plan's own verification step (the grep directive at the end of the bullet) is correct; the hard-coded list is what is wrong.
issues.evidence: grep -rln initialize_session_schema /home/john/elspeth/tests/ | sort | wc -l → 50 files; plan enumerates 9. Full list available via the grep command cited in the plan's own verification step.

issues.location_in_plan: §3 activation contract step 4a; Task 5 Goal paragraph; §8 NOT blocked by Postgres support
issues.claim: The plan says ELSPETH_WEB__MIGRATION_DRY_RUN and ELSPETH_WEB__MIGRATION_ORPHAN_SCAN_DAYS follow 'the Dynaconf ELSPETH_WEB__ nested-key convention used throughout the web subsystem.'
issues.reality: The ELSPETH_WEB__ prefix is confirmed as the project's web-subsystem convention for environment variable ingestion (src/elspeth/web/app.py line 366: prefix = 'ELSPETH_WEB__'; confirmed by extensive usage in playwright.config.ts and config.py). The new env vars ELSPETH_WEB__MIGRATION_DRY_RUN and ELSPETH_WEB__MIGRATION_ORPHAN_SCAN_DAYS correctly follow this convention. However, WebSettings (src/elspeth/web/config.py) does not yet declare these fields. For the Dynaconf injection pattern to work, these fields must be added to the WebSettings Pydantic model. The plan does not specify where in WebSettings these fields are declared.
issues.resolution: Task 3 or Task 5 should include an explicit sub-step: 'Add migration_dry_run: bool = False and migration_orphan_scan_days: int = 90 fields to WebSettings in src/elspeth/web/config.py.' Without this, the env vars are accepted by Dynaconf but ignored by the application.
issues.evidence: grep -n 'class WebSettings|landscape_url|session_db_url|MIGRATION' src/elspeth/web/config.py → WebSettings at line 29, landscape_url at line 136, session_db_url at line 180; no MIGRATION fields present

issues.location_in_plan: §3 activation contract: 'A @model_validator(mode='after') on WebSettings rejects configurations where landscape_url and session_db_url resolve to the same SQLite path.'
issues.claim: The plan proposes a new @model_validator(mode='after') on WebSettings for the same-path invariant.
issues.reality: WebSettings in src/elspeth/web/config.py already has multiple @model_validator(mode='after') validators (lines 371, 387, 432, 447, 462, 472). The pattern exists and is the correct approach. However, the existing validators at lines 450-458 already check landscape_url for SQLite-specific constraints (landscape_passphrase + non-SQLite URL rejection). Adding the same-path check follows established precedent. No blocker — the pattern is real and used. The plan's proposed validator is architecturally consistent. Flagged as a warning only because the plan does not state which existing validator it co-locates with, or whether it is a new named validator.
issues.resolution: Informational. No change required; the plan should specify the new validator is added as a new @model_validator(mode='after') after line 472 of config.py (the last existing one). The pydantic import already includes model_validator (line 8 of config.py).

issues.location_in_plan: §2 Option (c) classification table; §3 step 1; Task 1 bullet 'add info={"migration_class": "data"} to each Table per the classification table'; Task 2 TDD scenario (b) and (c) name 'user_preferences_table'
issues.claim: The plan uses both 'user_preferences_table' (Python variable name) and 'user_preferences' (SQL table name string) when referring to the same entity, without explicitly distinguishing them. In §3 step 1: 'Each Table carries an info["migration_class"] attribute.' In §2 classification table, the column header is 'Table' but entries mix string names ('sessions', 'chat_messages', etc.) with what could be read as either string names or variable names.
issues.reality: The Python variable name is user_preferences_table (src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py line 1238); the SQL table name string is 'user_preferences' (line 1239). In Task 2 scenario (b): 'live DB missing the entire user_preferences_table which is a data table' — this uses the variable name but the live DB would only contain the string 'user_preferences'. In the §2 classification table the column entries use short names ('sessions', 'user_preferences') which match the SQL table name strings, not the Python variable names. The inconsistency is localized but could confuse an implementer writing detector tests that check reflected table names against metadata.
issues.resolution: Choose one canonical form for §2's table column ('SQL table name string') and add a terminology note: 'Table names in this column are the SQL table name strings (matching metadata.tables.keys()); Python variable names append _table.' Update Task 2 scenario (b)/(c) to use string names consistently.
issues.evidence: src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py line 1238: 'user_preferences_table = Table('; line 1239: '"user_preferences"'; §2 classification table entries use bare names without _table suffix

issues.location_in_plan: Task 7 — 'Add an elspeth migration force-clear-orphan-plan <plan_id> subcommand to the existing elspeth CLI (NOT to elspeth-mcp, which is read-only).'
issues.claim: The plan proposes adding a 'migration' subcommand group to the elspeth CLI.
issues.reality: The elspeth CLI (src/elspeth/cli.py) uses typer.Typer with app.add_typer for subcommand groups (e.g. plugins_app = typer.Typer(...); app.add_typer(plugins_app, name='plugins') at lines 1230-1231). The pattern for adding a 'migration' subcommand group is consistent with how 'plugins' is structured. The plan is architecturally consistent. However, cli.py has no current 'migration' subcommand group — the new group is entirely new code. The claim that elspeth-mcp is read-only is confirmed (src/elspeth/mcp/server.py line 3: 'A lightweight read-only server'). No hallucination here, but the plan should specify the file to extend (src/elspeth/cli.py) and the typer.Typer pattern to follow.
issues.resolution: Add a note to Task 7 specifying the implementation location: src/elspeth/cli.py, following the plugins_app pattern at lines 1230-1231.
issues.evidence: grep -n 'add_typer|typer.Typer' src/elspeth/cli.py → plugins_app pattern at lines 1230-1231; src/elspeth/mcp/server.py line 3 confirms read-only

issues.location_in_plan: Task 9 Goal: 'boot the app against a DB at Phase 1A shape' — fixture table 'gen_1_phase_1a_only.sqlite' description: 'sessions, chat_messages, composition_states, user_preferences (no tutorial column)'
issues.claim: The plan's three-generation fixture description for gen_1 lists 'sessions, chat_messages, composition_states, user_preferences (no tutorial column)' — implying only 4 tables were present at Phase 1A time.
issues.reality: Phase 1A commit c01254145 ('feat(web): add user_preferences table to session schema') was not the initial schema commit. The initial schema commit (0cd5a7c18 'feat(web/sessions): add SQLAlchemy Core table definitions for session database') predates it. At Phase 1A, the sessions DB would also have had composition_proposals, proposal_events, interpretation_events (from Phase 5b commit 2e390fc0b), runs, blobs, blob_run_links, run_events, user_secrets, audit_access_log, and potentially others. The gen_1 fixture description is therefore incomplete and will produce a fixture that does not faithfully represent the actual schema at Phase 1A ship time. The fixture generation procedure (§5) acknowledges this by saying 'Checking out the git revision at which Phase 1A landed; booting the app; creating sessions and user preferences' but the gen_1 description column is misleading.
issues.resolution: Replace the abbreviated column descriptions in the Task 9 fixture table with a note: 'Tables present = all tables that existed at that phase's revision; see git log for exact schema.' The fixture generation procedure in §5 already correctly specifies checkout + boot; the abbreviated column description should not contradict it.
issues.evidence: git log --oneline RC5.2 -- src/elspeth/web/sessions/models.py shows commits predating Phase 1A that added the base table set; models.py HEAD comment lines 3-4: 'Tables: sessions, chat_messages, composition_states, runs, blobs, blob_run_links, user_secrets' were present before Phase 1A

issues.location_in_plan: Task 3 TDD shape: 'iterate dataclasses.fields(record) for each record dataclass; for every field whose type annotation is a Mapping or Sequence, assert isinstance(getattr(record, field.name), (types.MappingProxyType, tuple, frozenset))'
issues.claim: The proposed introspection test pattern checks for MappingProxyType, tuple, frozenset.
issues.reality: This matches the project's deep_freeze output types (src/elspeth/contracts/freeze.py): deep_freeze() converts dict/Mapping → MappingProxyType, list/Sequence → tuple, set → frozenset. The freeze_fields() function (line 78) applies deep_freeze to named fields. The proposed assertion pattern is consistent with how the project tests deep_freeze compliance elsewhere. No discrepancy.
issues.resolution: No change required. Pattern is correctly grounded.
issues.evidence: src/elspeth/contracts/freeze.py lines 46-65: deep_freeze output types are MappingProxyType (from Mapping/dict), tuple (from list/Sequence), frozenset (from set); freeze_fields at line 78 uses deep_freeze

risk_assessment.overall: Three of the four blocking issues (RR-001, RR-002, RR-004) are stale line numbers and an incomplete caller list — they cause implementation friction and commit failures but do not affect the architectural correctness of the plan. The fourth blocking issue (RR-003) is the most consequential: Phase 6's composer_completion_events_table and skill_markdown_history_table are both live in RC5.2 and absent from the §2 classification table. An implementer following the plan will produce a Task 1 commit that passes the 'every table has explicit classification' CI gate only by luck (if the gate counts metadata.tables.values() at runtime, it will see 15 tables and fail). More importantly, the §8 dependency list naming 'Phase 1A + Phase 5b' as the only BLOCKED-on phases is now stale: Phase 6 has already shipped to RC5.2, making this a non-blocking-for-implementation observation — Phase 6 is already present — but the plan's narrative framing of 'three schema-adding phases' is wrong (it's four, not three). The three-generation integration test in Task 9 should arguably become a four-generation test.
risk_assessment.if_blocking_issues_remain: RR-003 (missing table classifications) left unfixed will produce a caretaker that emits classification-default warnings for two tables on every deploy, making those warnings permanent noise rather than a signal for genuinely unclassified future tables. RR-004 (stale caller list) left unfixed will cause build failures when the delete commit is made, since 41+ test files will still import a deleted function. RR-001 and RR-002 (wrong line numbers) will cause the implementer to make changes at the wrong code locations.
